items.t: — off Wednesday's two-week peak of $4,165.87 as oil prints a six-week high and two-year yields a 17-month one; September hike odds hit 77%
— six jewelry HTS codes named, loose polished diamonds included, effective August 19 under Section 338; rough exempt, USMCA overridden
— USTR's expedited 301 proposes the rate on colored stones and cultured pearls from ~46 countries; resolution due by month-end
— the temporary 10% global import surcharge expires July 24; importers with goods in transit get a rare clean window
— constant-currency H1 growth on CHF 3.12B revenue, US +27%, China +9%; net profit CHF 16M — half a percent of sales
— the annual loss narrows to CAD 3.4M on sales up 15.5% to CAD 205.4M; a Birks mono-brand store lands at Vancouver's Oakridge this fall
— Monica Rich Kosann's Enchanted Garden tops out at a special-order lemon with white-diamond seeds; the cherry starts at $10,985
— Liljenquist & Beckstead opens a Rolex boutique on the Galleria's main corridor, Rolex-trained workshop set behind glass
— the 1999 Résonance 022/99R heads to Phillips Geneva Nov 7–8 with a CHF 1M high estimate, its first public appearance in 27 years
— the white metals hand back a slice of Tuesday: silver −1.3%, platinum −1.0% to $1,628.63, palladium −1.2% to $1,274.96
